intro: Wednesday brings a wave of frontier model launches: Anthropic's 10-trillion-parameter Claude Mythos 5, Google's 2-million-token Gemini 3.1 Ultra, and OpenAI doubling down on autonomous computer-use. Behind the headlines, Q1 VC deal value hits a record $267B — almost entirely AI.

top_stories.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude Mythos 5 — First 10-Trillion-Parameter Model
top_stories.summary: Anthropic has released Claude Mythos 5, the first publicly acknowledged 10-trillion-parameter model. Engineered for high-stakes domains — cybersecurity, academic research, and complex coding — the model raises the bar on reasoning depth and sets a new scale benchmark for the industry. Anthropic has now surpassed $19B in annualised revenue.

top_stories.headline: Google Ships Gemini 3.1 Ultra with 2M-Token Context Window
top_stories.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Ultra arrives with a 2-million-token native multimodal context window covering text, image, audio, and video. Alongside it, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite promises 2.5× faster responses at just $0.25 per million input tokens — putting affordable long-context inference within reach for most developers.

top_stories.headline: GPT-5.4 Gains Native Computer-Use: Agents Can Now Navigate Files, Browsers and Terminals
top_stories.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 series is the first general-purpose model with state-of-the-art native computer-use capabilities. The model can autonomously navigate operating systems, browsers, and terminals — and has already surpassed human-level performance on desktop task benchmarks, moving agentic AI from demo to deployment.

top_stories.headline: Google TurboQuant Slashes KV-Cache Memory at ICLR 2026
top_stories.summary: Google researchers unveiled TurboQuant at ICLR 2026, a two-step algorithm combining PolarQuant vector rotation with Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss compression to dramatically reduce KV-cache memory overhead. The technique could unlock longer context inference on existing hardware without requiring new chips.

top_stories.headline: Q1 2026 Venture Deal Value Hits Record $267B — AI Dominates
top_stories.summary: Global venture deal value reached $267.2 billion in Q1 2026, with AI accounting for the lion's share. OpenAI alone raised $122 billion and Anthropic closed a $30 billion Series G. OpenAI is also taking early steps toward a public listing as its annualised revenue crosses $25 billion.

quick_hits.text: Coherent Corp expands its Nvidia supply deal following a breakthrough in 400 Gbps silicon photonics, accelerating data transfer inside AI clusters.
Gartner: only 28% of AI projects in infrastructure and operations deliver meaningful returns; 20% fail outright. ROI discipline is now the dominant enterprise conversation.
NVIDIA spotlights physical AI progress during National Robotics Week — latest research covers manipulation, locomotion, and sim-to-real transfer.
Morgan Stanley warns most of the world isn't ready for the next AI capability leap — citing infrastructure gaps, regulatory lag, and workforce readiness.
Six AI breakthroughs InfoWorld says will define 2026: multimodal reasoning, autonomous agents, efficient inference, synthetic data, AI security, and physical AI.

india_roundup.text: Yotta Data Services is building Asia's largest AI computing hub using Nvidia Blackwell Ultra chips, with a planned IPO by early 2027.
Macquarie flags India as a potential AI powerhouse: the country ranks 3rd globally in AI funding (2.3% of global capital) and 4th in AI companies founded since 2008.
Sarvam AI continues development of India's sovereign AI model — aiming to deliver a foundation model trained on Indian languages and data.
Indian enterprise AI adoption accelerates: 47% of organisations now have multiple use cases live, and nearly half have moved over 21% of their GenAI pilots into production.
IndiaAI Mission's Centres of Excellence push inclusive AI — targeting open, affordable access in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities as the next frontier.

closing_insight: We are past the era of AI as a research project. Three frontier labs shipped major models on the same day, VC deployed a quarter-trillion dollars in a single quarter, and enterprises are now judged not on whether they adopted AI but on whether they made it pay. The competitive edge has shifted from access to execution.
